screen: Landing — Hero Banner & Value Proposition Cards
finding: Landing page copy exclusively promotes upside — 'Turn your foreign currency needs into potential for better returns', 'making your capital work harder for you' — with zero mention of risk for a capital-at-risk structured product with embedded derivatives.
novice_impact: Forms an expectation of guaranteed positive returns similar to a savings deposit; no signal that principal could be converted to a less favourable currency.
seasoned_impact: Recognises the promotional tone but notes the absence of legally required risk disclaimers at point of entry.
recommendation: Add a visible risk disclaimer directly below the hero banner: 'DCI is a structured investment product. Your principal may be converted to the alternate currency at maturity. This is not a deposit and is not covered by deposit insurance.' Balance every return claim with a risk counterpoint.

finding: Risk disclosures are buried exclusively in a collapsed FAQ section on the landing page. A user can configure, review, and submit a structured investment order without encountering any risk warning in the flow.
novice_impact: Has no opportunity to understand downside risk before committing capital; may not realise they could receive a different currency.
seasoned_impact: Expects to see risk disclosures inline and may question the product's regulatory compliance.
recommendation: Insert a risk summary card on the configuration screen (Step 1) above the strike price selection. Show key risks: (1) principal may settle in alternate currency, (2) not a deposit / not insured, (3) funds locked until maturity. Require a risk acknowledgment checkbox on the review screen before enabling Submit.

finding: No suitability assessment or Customer Knowledge Assessment (CKA) before allowing investment in a structured product with embedded derivatives. MAS regulations for Specified Investment Products typically require this.
novice_impact: Allowed to invest in a complex product they may not understand, with no gate to verify comprehension.
seasoned_impact: May question whether the platform meets regulatory requirements, eroding trust.
recommendation: Add a suitability gate before the configuration screen: (1) Confirm user understands DCI is not a deposit, (2) Confirm user understands settlement may be in alternate currency, (3) Confirm user has read the product factsheet. Block progression until acknowledgments are complete.

screen: Investment Details — Strike Price Not Met
finding: Settlement outcome text states 'converted to 281,332.90 SGD' but the settlement breakdown table totals 280,735.90 SGD — a 597 SGD discrepancy in a financial product where accuracy is paramount.
novice_impact: Sees two different numbers for the same value, destroying confidence in the platform's accuracy.
seasoned_impact: Immediately flags the inconsistency as a potential calculation error; may escalate to relationship manager.
recommendation: Audit all calculated values for consistency between narrative text and data tables. Implement automated validation to ensure displayed amounts match across all presentation formats on the same screen.

finding: Annualized yield (e.g., '6.50% p.a.') is the primary visual anchor for strike price selection. For a 1-month tenor, actual return is ~0.54%; for a 1-week tenor, ~0.125%. The actual period return is never shown explicitly.
novice_impact: Perceives 6.50% as the return they will receive, not understanding the annualisation. Dramatically inflated expectations.
seasoned_impact: Understands p.a. convention but would still benefit from seeing actual period return for quick comparison.
recommendation: Show both annualized and actual period return: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)' or display actual dollar return with equal or greater visual prominence than the annualized percentage.

finding: Strike price options display increasing yields as strike price moves further from spot, but the tradeoff — higher yield means greater probability of alternate currency settlement — is never explained.
novice_impact: Naturally selects the highest yield option without understanding they are accepting greater conversion risk.
seasoned_impact: Understands the tradeoff implicitly but the absence of explanation may concern them about how novice users are protected.
recommendation: Add a brief explanatory note below the strike price options: 'A strike price further from the spot rate offers higher yield but increases the chance of settlement in the alternate currency.' Consider a visual risk-reward indicator (e.g., a scale or gradient).

finding: No explicit T&C consent checkbox. The review screen uses implicit consent: 'By clicking Submit, you confirm that you have read, understood and agree to the Terms and Conditions.' For a structured product with embedded derivatives, implicit consent may not meet regulatory requirements.
novice_impact: May not read the disclaimer text above the Submit button; unknowingly consents to terms they haven't reviewed.
seasoned_impact: Expects an explicit consent mechanism for a structured product; implicit consent feels rushed.
recommendation: Replace implicit consent with an explicit checkbox: '☐ I have read and agree to the Terms and Conditions' that must be checked before Submit is enabled. Add a second checkbox for risk acknowledgment: '☐ I understand this investment may settle in the alternate currency.'

finding: Navigation label changes from 'FX and Investment' (landing, config, review, confirmation) to 'FX and Treasury' (investment detail screens). The OMC gate screen incorrectly highlights 'Trade Finance'. Three different nav states across one product flow.
novice_impact: Believes they have navigated to a different product area; loses orientation within the application.
seasoned_impact: Recognises the inconsistency as a design defect; erodes trust in the platform's attention to detail.
recommendation: Standardise on 'FX and Investment' across all DCI-related screens. Ensure the correct nav item is highlighted regardless of entry point (direct link, reinvest, or OMC redirect).

finding: Helper text displays 'Minimum amount $0,000.00 USD' — a template placeholder that was never replaced with the actual minimum investment amount.
novice_impact: Cannot determine the minimum investment amount; '$0,000.00' is confusing and looks broken.
seasoned_impact: Recognises it as a placeholder but questions the product's readiness for production.
recommendation: Replace with the actual minimum investment amount (e.g., 'Minimum amount: 10,000.00 USD'). If the minimum varies by currency, make this dynamic based on the selected currency.

screen: Reinvest Config — Strike Price Not Met
finding: When reinvesting after a 'Strike Price Not Met' outcome, the currency pair silently flips (e.g., EUR/SGD becomes SGD/EUR). The base currency changes without any contextual explanation of why this happened.
novice_impact: Confused by the currency change; may not understand why their base currency is different from their original investment.
seasoned_impact: Understands the mechanics but would prefer a clear contextual note confirming the currency switch.
recommendation: Add a contextual banner at the top of the reinvestment configuration: 'Your previous investment settled in SGD (alternate currency). Your new investment uses SGD as the base currency.' Optionally allow users to switch the currency pair direction.

screen: Config, Review, Confirmation — Primary Buttons & Badges
finding: OCBC brand red is overloaded — used simultaneously for primary CTA buttons (Submit, Next), 'Base Currency' scenario badges, navigation accents, and 'Add' buttons. In banking UI, red universally signals danger or error, creating subconscious tension when used for positive actions.
novice_impact: May hesitate before clicking red buttons on a financial submission page. Red 'Base Currency' badge may be misread as a warning.
seasoned_impact: Less confused by button colour but finds red 'Base Currency' badge semantically incorrect — green would be more intuitive for the favourable outcome.
recommendation: Consider using a non-red colour (e.g., the dark teal already in the system) for primary CTAs, reserving red for errors/warnings only. At minimum, change the 'Base Currency' badge from red to a neutral or positive colour (blue or green).

finding: Financial jargon used throughout the configuration screen without tooltips or inline explanations: 'Tenor' (should be 'Investment Period'), 'Strike Price', 'Fixing Rate', 'Settlement Currency', 'Base Currency', 'Alternate Currency'. FAQ definitions exist but are on a separate page.
novice_impact: Cannot make informed decisions because key terms are undefined; average novice comprehensibility score is 2.3/5.
seasoned_impact: No issue — terminology is standard for structured products (4.8/5 comprehensibility).
recommendation: Add info (ⓘ) icons with tooltip definitions next to each financial term on the configuration screen. Use plain-language labels where possible: 'Tenor' → 'Investment Period', 'Fixing Rate' → 'Market Rate at Expiry'. Keep technical terms in parentheses for seasoned users.

finding: Scenario A (base currency, strike met) uses green badge/tint; Scenario B (alternate currency, strike not met) uses orange badge/tint. Green universally implies 'good' and orange implies 'caution', but Scenario B may be the user's preferred outcome if they need the alternate currency.
novice_impact: Unconsciously perceives Scenario A as 'correct' and Scenario B as 'bad', regardless of their actual currency needs.
seasoned_impact: May notice the colour bias but is not significantly affected in decision-making.
recommendation: Use neutral, non-judgmental colours for both scenarios (e.g., blue and slate, or two different neutral tones). Alternatively, let the user declare their preferred outcome and highlight that scenario in green.

finding: The review screen requires extensive scrolling — Investment Details, Accounts, Potential Outcomes (two scenario cards), Investment Timeline, T&C disclaimer — before reaching the Submit button. No sticky CTA or floating summary bar is provided.
novice_impact: May not realise they need to scroll further to find Submit; long page feels overwhelming before a financial commitment.
seasoned_impact: Finds the scrolling tedious for repeat investments; expects a summary panel or sticky footer.
recommendation: Add a sticky bottom bar showing key summary (amount, currency pair, yield) with the Submit button always visible. Alternatively, use collapsible sections so users can expand only the details they want to re-check.

finding: Terms and Conditions modal presents legal text as an undifferentiated wall of text with no visible headings, bullet points, or section breaks. Users cannot scan for relevant sections.
novice_impact: Will not read any of the T&C; loses the opportunity to understand key risks from the legal document.
seasoned_impact: Cannot quickly locate specific clauses they care about (e.g., early termination, force majeure).
recommendation: Structure T&C content with section headings (bold), numbered clauses, and bullet points for key risks. Highlight the 3-5 most important clauses (capital risk, currency conversion, no deposit insurance) with visual emphasis.

finding: No step progress indicator in the 3-step investment flow (Configure → Accounts → Review). The 'REVIEW' sidebar label appears only on the review screen; no equivalent labels on other steps. Users cannot gauge how many steps remain.
novice_impact: Uncertain how much longer the process will take; may abandon if they perceive it as open-ended.
seasoned_impact: Minor — experienced users can infer the step count, but still expect a stepper component in a multi-step financial flow.
recommendation: Add a horizontal stepper component showing: Step 1: Configure → Step 2: Accounts → Step 3: Review → Confirmation. Display it consistently across all steps.

finding: The OMC (Open Multi-Currency Account) gate screen shows 'Trade Finance' as the active navigation item instead of 'FX and Investment', breaking user orientation.
novice_impact: Believes they've been redirected to a different product area; confused about how to return to DCI.
seasoned_impact: Recognises the navigation error but it reinforces the impression of an inconsistent product.
recommendation: Ensure the OMC gate screen inherits the 'FX and Investment' navigation state. Add a breadcrumb or back link: 'Dual Currency Investment > Account Required'.

finding: Both the spot rate and yield display the qualifier 'Indicative' without explaining what it means — 'Current Spot Rate (Indicative)' and 'Indicative yield'. Users don't know if rates are guaranteed, estimated, or subject to change.
novice_impact: Uncertain whether the displayed yield is what they'll actually receive; 'Indicative' creates anxiety without explanation.
seasoned_impact: Understands that rates are live quotes subject to market movement, but the lack of a timestamp refresh mechanism for yield is noted.
recommendation: Add a tooltip or inline note: 'Indicative rates are based on current market conditions and may change before your order is confirmed. The final rate will be locked at submission.' Show a clear timestamp for when rates were last refreshed.

finding: 'Place Another Investment' is presented as the primary CTA immediately after placing an investment, potentially encouraging impulsive repeat investment without pause for reflection.
novice_impact: May feel prompted to reinvest immediately without evaluating whether the first investment aligns with their overall portfolio strategy.
seasoned_impact: Appreciates quick access to place another investment but recognises the nudge.
recommendation: Make 'Return to Dashboard' the primary CTA and 'Place Another Investment' a secondary (outline) button. This gives users a natural pause between investment decisions.

finding: The 'Add New Currency' modal uses a dark teal/slate primary button while all other screens use OCBC red for primary actions. Two different primary button colours exist in the design system.
novice_impact: Minimal — may briefly notice the different button colour.
seasoned_impact: Minimal — recognises it as a design inconsistency.
recommendation: Unify primary button colour across all modals and screens. Use OCBC red consistently for primary CTAs, or adopt dark teal system-wide.

finding: Expanded confirmation repeats nearly all information from the review screen (investment details, accounts, potential outcomes). At the confirmation stage, users need reassurance and a reference number — not a full investment recap.
novice_impact: May re-read all information looking for something they missed, increasing cognitive load at a moment that should bring relief.
seasoned_impact: Will skip the detail; expanded view is wasted screen real estate.
recommendation: Default to collapsed confirmation (reference number + timeline + next action). Provide an expandable 'View full details' section for users who want the complete recap.

finding: 'p.a.' abbreviation used throughout yield displays without ever being expanded to 'per annum' or 'per year'. Combined with inconsistent case conventions (ALL CAPS vs title case for section headers).
novice_impact: May not know what 'p.a.' means; combined with annualized yield, this compounds the comprehension gap.
seasoned_impact: No issue — standard financial abbreviation.
recommendation: Expand 'p.a.' on first use: '6.50% p.a. (per year)'. Standardise heading case conventions — use ALL CAPS letter-spaced for page-level identifiers and title case for section headings consistently.
